channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Samuel L. Jackson lauded at MoMA film benefit by close family and friends
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Museum of Modern Art was filled with expletives and laughter Wednesday evening as Samuel L. Jackson’s family and friends celebrated the actor and director’s storied career of box office hits, larger-than-life characters and explosive one-liners at the annual film benefit.
item.dc:creator: BEATRICE DUPUY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Colleges left helpless as students rule out schools due to state politics
item.description: Colleges and universities find themselves increasingly trapped with the politics of the state where they reside.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No end for Boeing labor strike as workers reject latest contract proposal
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Boeing factory workers have voted against the company’s latest contract offer and will remain on the picket lines six weeks into a strike that has stopped production of the aerospace giant’s bestselling jetliners.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: EPA imposes stricter standards to protect children from exposure to lead paint
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Two weeks after setting a nationwide deadline for removal of lead pipes, the Biden administration is imposing strict new limits on dust from lead-based paint in older homes and child-care facilities.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Letter at center of Menendez brothers' bid for freedom called into question
item.description: Kitty Menendez's brother questions letter at heart of Lyle and Erik Menendez's appeal for freedom despite life without parole for shotgun murder of their parents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Carolina lawmakers convene again to address Hurricane Helene's billions in damages
item.description: North Carolina state legislators are returning to work to consider further relief for recovery from Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Gary D. Robertson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Funeral home owners accused of storing decaying bodies expected to plead guilty to COVID-19 fraud
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Colorado funeral home owners accused of misspending nearly $900,000 in COVID-19 pandemic relief funds and living lavishly, all while allegedly stashing 190 decaying bodies in a building and sending grieving families fake ashes, are expected to plead guilty to federal charges Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia men who killed Ahmaud Arbery want a new trial
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — Three white men serving life in prison for murder after chasing and killing Ahmaud Arbery in 2020 are returning to court Thursday to ask a judge for a new trial.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Abortion bans are top of mind for young women in North Carolina as they consider Harris or Trump
item.description: TikTok videos of women turned away from emergency rooms because of state abortion bans haunt one young female voter in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz And Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: One in five neighbors have fought over politics: Survey
item.description: Among those surveyed, men were more than twice as likely to get into a dispute than women, according to Redfin.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia, candidates target of ‘sustained’ cyberattacks: Reports
item.description: CNN says Georgia fended off the likely overseas attack, while Microsoft says China, Russia and Iran are targeting the U.S. election process.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Before Taylor Swift show in New Orleans, a homeless encampment is forced to move
item.description: In anticipation of three Taylor Swift concerts, which could draw around 150,000 visitors to New Orleans' Superdome this weekend, state authorities on Wednesday began clearing a nearby homeless encampment.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida mailman stabs lawncare worker over parking: deputies
item.description: The Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office has released more information about the events that may have led up to a deadly stabbing Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TV host Adam Richman says production crew robbed while filming in North London
item.description: TV host Adam Richman, best known for starring in "Man v. Food," says he and his production crew were robbed while filming a show in the UK.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A man who threatened to kill Democratic election officials pleads guilty
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A Colorado man repeatedly made online threats about killing the top elections officials in his state and Arizona — both Democrats — as well as a judge and law enforcement agents, according to a guilty plea he entered Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ronna McDaniel: How Republicans can win Gen Z voters
item.description: In an election that will be won on the margins, the failure of Republicans to engage these new young voters would amount to a missed opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Ronna McDaniel, opinion contributor, NewsNation

item.title: Shohei Ohtani’s 50/50 ball shatters record for most expensive in sports history
item.description: Ohtani became the first player in the sport’s history to hit 50 home runs and steal 50 bases in a season, doing so on Sept. 19 against the Miami Marlins.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. Dept. of Transportation levies $50 million fine against American Airlines
item.description: The US Department of Transportation has fined American Airlines $50 million for violating the rights of people with disabilities, including unsafe physical assistance resulting in injuries and undignified treatment of wheelchair users.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Laredo lifts boil water order after E. coli 'crisis' infiltrated city's water
item.description: he South Texas border city of Laredo has lifted its boil-water order after dangerous E. coli was found in the city's water system, which has been blamed on illegal or faulty water connections.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Democrat raises alarm over Trump campaign's failure to enter into transition agreements
item.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-Md.) is raising concerns about the Trump campaign’s failure to enter into presidential transition agreements with the federal government, cautioning that the delay could negatively impact the transfer of power early next year.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Quarter of young Black men say they will vote for Trump: Survey
item.description: Despite one-fourth of young Black men saying they would cast their ballot for Trump, they repeatedly identify Harris as the strongest candidate on the issues.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Goldfish changes name of iconic crackers to appeal to adults
item.description: Snack lovers can look forward to a playful twist on a childhood favorite, as Goldfish announces a limited-time renaming of its iconic Cheddar crackers.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, KRON4

item.title: Suspected car thief dead after high-speed chase, crash on live TV
item.description: A woman died after she was possibly ejected from the vehicle she was driving following a lengthy pursuit that ended in a high-speed crash on the 5 Freeway Wednesday afternoon. The pursuit began with the Los Angeles Police Department following an allegedly stolen Honda CR-V at about 1:45 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pilot Chuck Coleman killed in air show crash in Las Cruces
item.description: Coleman was an aerobatic flight instructor for actors in the 2022 motion picture, "Top Gun: Maverick," where he flew more than 100 flights preparing actors to fly in Navy F-18 Hornets.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Well-known pilot Chuck Coleman killed in air show crash in Las Cruces
item.description: The pilot who was killed in a crash at a Las Cruces air show over the weekend has been identified as Charles Thomas "Chuck" Coleman of California, the City of Las Cruces said in an update issued Monday afternoon, Oct. 21.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, Fox 5

item.title: DNA links a suspected Chicago-area serial killer who died in 1981 to a woman's 1979 death
item.description: NORTH AURORA, Ill. (AP) — DNA found on the clothing of a suburban Chicago woman who was kidnapped and killed 45 years ago matches that of a suspected serial killer who died two years later, authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Kristy strengthens into a Category 4 storm in the Pacific Ocean
item.description: Hurricane Kristy strengthened into a Category 4 storm in the Pacific Ocean on Wednesday and was expected to remain away from land as it churns in the Pacific Ocean, forecasters said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Carville lists 3 reasons why he thinks Harris will win
item.description: “The biggest reason Mr. Trump will lose is that the whole Republican Party has been on a losing streak since Mr. Trump took it over,” he said in an essay published Wednesday by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: They made 36 million Corn Poppers. Here's how they pick the right ones for the Toy Hall of Fame
item.description: ROCHESTER, N.Y. (AP) — When curators at the National Toy Hall of Fame learned last fall that the Fisher-Price Corn Popper had been voted in as part of the class of 2023, they knew they had some serious work to do.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: USPS urges voters not to delay mailing their ballots
item.description: Top officials at the U.S. Postal Service are urging voters to mail in their ballots at least one week before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, Fox 5

item.title: Suspected drunken driver arrested after passing Harris' motorcade the wrong way in Wisconsin
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A suspected drunken driver heading the wrong way passed within feet of Vice President Kamala Harris' motorcade following a campaign stop this week in Wisconsin.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Colorado girl, 8, fights off attacker wearing clown mask in her bedroom
item.description: A Colorado man is accused of assaulting an 8-year-old girl in her bedroom but fled when she fought back, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video: Suspected drunken driver passes within feet of Harris' motorcade in Wisconsin
item.description: The driver was traveling in the wrong direction on the interstate in Milwaukee when deputies spotted him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Over 7,100 bottles of prescription antidepressants recalled over potentially cancer-causing substance
item.description: A pharmaceutical company issued a recall this month for thousands of bottles of duloxetine, a medicine often prescribed to treat depression.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tennessee man had enough after SUV broken into 5 times in 3 years
item.description: "I have been in the Home Depot parking sleeping because I'm out of hours and I'm hearing noise. They were out there doing donuts and breaking into cars," said D'Juan Terry.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, NewsNation

item.title: Man charged with stealing $5K worth of electric scooters from Vanderbilt University
item.description: Officials said Harold Anderson, 65, "implicated himself" in multiple scooter thefts, and then requested an attorney.
item.dc:creator: Aubriella Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: Harvey Weinstein back in New York court for hearing ahead of retrial in sex crime case
item.description: Harvey Weinstein is back in court in New York for a hearing ahead of his retrial on rape charges stemming from his landmark #MeToo case, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
